wetting theory for solders and brazes

captioner_flags: p1: 'tungsten lium' — likely 'tellurium' but uncertain; left as '[tellurium?]'
notes: Lecture covers wrap-up of dissimilar metal thermal expansion, transitions through wetting/flux theory with three classic failure modes (oxalic acid, glucose, abietic acid), digresses memorably into rosin-baked potatoes and Texas Roadhouse, then closes with two operations-research case studies (Motorola pager plant, Ford Mustang plant) illustrating that the 6% industry rework rate was self-fulfilling. Lecture ends with 'see you tomorrow' — part of a tightly serialized sequence.
